summary: Installs persistent SYSTEM/root service with remote command channel and 6-hour auto-update from npm @latest
details: On Windows, scripts/post-install.js registers a Windows service that runs service/daemon.js as SYSTEM with auto-start and auto-restart. service/updater.js runs `npm install -g claw-subagent-service@<latest>` on a 6-hour interval inside that privileged daemon, so any future version published to the npm tag is fetched and re-executed as SYSTEM without further user consent. service/worker.js connects to a hardcoded RongCloud IM endpoint (https://newsradar.dreamdt.cn/im, AppKey 'bmdehs6pbyyks') and dispatches incoming COMMAND / DEVICE_CONTROL / CHAT_MESSAGE messages to handlers in service/modules/script-executor.js that spawn arbitrary `cmd` / `bash` processes, install/uninstall the service, and run `openclaw doctor --fix`. The combination is a vendor-controlled remote-shell channel running at SYSTEM/root plus a self-updating delivery path: anyone who controls the npm publish token or the RongCloud IM tenant can execute arbitrary code as SYSTEM/root on every host that has installed this package. The Linux/macOS path additionally writes a systemd unit / LaunchDaemon plist named 'SilentNodeService' with a generic 'silent background service' description (Chinese: 'Node.js 静默后台服务'), obscuring the package origin in service listings (gated behind user-invoked `--install`, not postinstall).

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
